title: nassella macrotricha
intro: Nassella macrotricha, also known as Mackinlay's button grass or button grass, is a species of grass native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain a compound called sesquiterpene, that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Nassella macrotricha include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Nassella macrotricha can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
